The most imposing is the Valle de los Caídos, the Valley of the Fallen, incorporating a huge basilica built into the side of a mountain. War memorials and plaques commemorating those who had died in the Spanish Civil War were installed in many towns and villages. texts.full_text: anical cavity. We demonstrate the actuation of a double beam opto-mechanical cavities using low-power laser radiation. We show that, at variance with conventional techniques based on photothermal effects or parametric driving, our method is not affected by mechanical instabilities and can be used to resonantly excite any vibrational mode up-to frequencies in the MHz range without inducing unwanted heating of these nanostructures. We study a new class of optomechanical systems where light confinement arises from an optical cavity formed between two high reflectivity mirrors. The central element consists on one side mirror attached onto mechanical nanoresonator, which is free to oscillate under thermal or external forces in the presence and absence respectively of laser radiation . We show that by resonantly exciting a vibrational mode of such nano-resoantor with low power optical field it will be possible not only control its motion but also cool down this vibration using standard side-band cooling

texts.full_text: as fractionated in 12 subsets (isoelectric focusing; fractions of the peptides ranging from pH 3--10 [@b0025]), thereby increasing the "depth" of peptide identification. To rule out that highly abundant proteins are falsely selected as RBPs (potential false positives), we included staining for proteins such as tubulin and histones in our western blots, serving as negative controls since these proteins are not expected to bind to RNA ([Fig. 2](#f0010){ref-type="fig"}C,D). Additionally, we used the yeast RBP data obtained from [@b0025] and compared the cellular levels of identified proteins (taken from [@b0130]) with the enrichment in interactome capture recovery after crosslinking compared to the non-crosslinking control ([Fig. 3](#f0015){ref-type="fig"}B). We observe no correlation between overall protein abundance and enrichment in interactome capture, arguing against a favoured selection of abundant proteins in our protocol. We and others have previously used 254 nm UV light to crosslink RNA and proteins in an RNA-labeling independent manner (cCL) [@b0055], [@b0175], [@b0025]. Recently, two laboratories have used this approach to investigate RBPs in yeast in a similar manner [@b0225], [@b0200]. Comparison of interactome capture data originated from either cCL (120 RBPs [@b0220]; 765 RBPs [@b0200]) and PAR-CL (678 RBPs [@b0025]) in yeast which yield 417 (cCL) and 299 (PAR-CL) RBPs exclusively ([Fig. 3](#f0015){ref-type="fig"} C) indicates that the afforementioned features of RBPs might not play a pivotal role for capture of an RBP by interactome capture with a high enrichment score -- or if it can be captured with one of the two irradiation techniques at all. The overlap between RBPs captured with both UV crosslinking approaches is only ∼35% which is rather low when compared to human interactomes in which 64% and 65% of the RBPs are detected using UV light at both wavelengths in HeLa cells [@b0055] or HuH-7 cells [@b0025], respectively. However, the yeast studies have been performed using different background correction (non-crosslink control vs. poly (A) RNA removal) and a different mass spectrometry approach which might contribute to the bigger diversity in commonly detected RBPs. texts.full_text: ed for light microscopy with a fluorescence microscope and is used primarily on microbiological samples. This technique uses the specificity of antibodies to their antigen to target fluorescent dyes to specific biomolecule targets within a cell, and therefore allows visualization of the distribution of the target molecule through the sample. The specific region an antibody recognizes on an antigen is called an epitope. There have been efforts in epitope mapping since many antibodies can bind the same epitope and levels of binding between antibodies that recognize the same epitope can vary. Additionally, the binding of the fluorophore to the antibody itself cannot interfere with the immunological specificity of the antibody or the binding capacity of its antigen. Immunofluorescence is a widely used example of immunostaining (using antibodies to stain proteins) and is a specific example of immunohistochemistry (the use of the antibody-antigen relationship in tissues). This technique primarily makes use of fluoroof- and fluoresce- antibodies. texts.full_text: elated Art Generally, the cutting blade is attached to a pattern for use in pressing a folding or a cutting line on plate matters such as paper, canvas, leather, plastic, etc. The plate matters with such pressed lines can be used in a folded shape like a box. Accordingly, in order to assemble and process the plate matter into a predetermined box shape with the cutting blade, it is necessary to perform two separate operations: the cutting operation to create the outline and the folding operation to form the fold lines. This traditional method is time-consuming and requires multiple steps, including the alignment of the plate matter for each operation, which can lead to inaccuracies and inefficiencies in the manufacturing process. To overcome these limitations, various methods and apparatuses have been proposed.

This is one of those scenes that make me realize, even after 25 years of trying to write comedy, I don’t always understand what’s funny. And I’m forced to admit the actors and the director sometimes know better than I do. In this case, they took a two-minute scene as written and turned it into five minutes where the audience laughs non-stop. When Johnny finally catches on and breaks out of his cell, he sings this celebratory reprise. DAVID: For me, this is an example of the value of a reprise, even when used as underscoring. At the beginning of this, Johnny is feeling such despair at the prospect of hanging that he fails to notice that the sheriff has left him a key with which to get out of his cell, and a horse saddled out back with which to make his escape. Tower of Orthanc will not be fully constructed; its stone blocks are unevenly stacked with gaps visible between them. The shell station's metal panels glisten in the sunlight but lack some crucial parts—pipes jut out haphazardly without connections, fuel pumps sit idle and disconnected from any machinery below ground, and warning signs hang loosely on crooked poles. The once-majestic Tower of Orthanc rises partially into the sky like a broken toothpick stuck through an apple. Its sides are covered in scaffolding that wavers precariously against the wind as workers scramble up ladders to fix stones or add new ones without proper mortar between them, leaving large cracks exposed where water could easily seep inside. A third cookie will be created if you click on the "Remember me" checkbox, and will contain a longer-lasting session identifier (hereinafter “long-session-id”), which will keep you logged in for a longer period or until you explicitly log out. These cookies are essential for the proper functioning of the forum and for you to be able to use its features. Secondly, when you register and create an account on “freebasic.net”, you provide certain personal information, such as your username, email address, and a password. As a workaround for this, in Highcharts all text options are accompanied by an additional option, `useHTML`. When using HTML, all browsers handle bidirectional text without a problem. Note that in order for this to show correctly on exported charts, you need to set the [exporting.allowHTML](https://api.highchartCertainly! Here's a continuation and clarification of the provided text regarding Highcharts' internationalization features, with practical examples and explanations: --- ### Reversed and BiDi Text (Arabic, Hebrew) Highcharts supports right-to-left (RTL) languages like Arabic and Hebrew through bidirectional (BiDi) text handling. Modern browsers manage BiDi text in SVG, but complex HTML strings in SVG can cause issues. To resolve this: #### 1. Use `useHTML: true` for Text Elements Set `useHTML: true` in text-related options (e.g., labels, tooltips) to render text as HTML instead of SVG. This ensures correct BiDi rendering in browsers. texts.full_text: data points you may consider tracking in order to prevent attacks or analyze website behavior: 1. IP Address: This is one of the most basic pieces information that can be tracked. 2. User Agent String (Browser details): This helps identify what browser, operating system etc., a user uses on your site 3. Geolocation Data : Provides insights into where users are coming from geographically and network-wise 4. Device Information: Identifies if the device is mobile or desktop based. However tracking these attributes should be done in compliance with privacy laws such as GDPR, CCPA etc., to prevent any legal issues. The tracked data can indeed help identify unusual patterns that could signify an attack (like sudden spikes from unfamiliar IP addresses), but remember this alone isn't a silver bullet. You still need robust security measures like encryption and secure authentication systems in place. Creating log files of database updates is also good practice as it helps to maintain records for auditing purposes, can help track data corruption or unauthorized changes. texts.full_text: Skip to content Anna Woofenden Menu Home Book Study Guide Praise for “This is God’s Table” Media Podcast Offerings Recent Sermons Blog Speaking Events Contact Tag: God with Us Posted on January 20, 2014 God Pitched Her Tent Among Us Sermon for Saint Gregory of Nyssa Episcopal Church San Francisco, California December 29, 2013 Text: John 1:1-18 Audio:https://annawoofenden.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/01/131229_0830_woofenden.mp3 Last weekend my siblings and I flew and drove in from all over the country, to gather on Guemes Island in Washington State, at our childhood home. We gathered to surround our mother as she said goodbye to her home of 30+ years, the home which my parents built, the gardens she’s worked, the field that housed various cows, pigs, and even a few goats over the years, the property where all seven of us, her children, were raised. “Momentous,” is the word a friend used to expressed this move. Momentous to be saying goodbye to the place where so many memories have been made; momentuous because it marks such significant change for our mother's life and ours as well. As I stood on that land, looking out at the water, feeling the wind in my hair, smelling salt air mixed with wood smoke...I couldn't help but think of another home-going: God pitching her tent

texts.full_text: icle. The road's end is also close to the southern limit of Nunavik, Quebec's Inuit region which is negotiating for autonomous status within Quebec. The Trans-Taiga Road branches off from the James Bay Road () at kilometer 544. It was built as an access road to the hydro-electric generating stations of Hydro-Québec along the La Grande River and Caniapiscau River. Several outfitters are established along this road, providing wilderness hunting and fishing expeditions, and some may also provide fuel, food and lodging. Otherwise, there are no services along this road. While the Trans-Taiga Road is reportedly drivable by ordinary passenger vehicles for its entire length, Hydro-Québec recommends use of 4WD vehicles for the section between Brisay and Caniapiscau, which is of a rougher grade than the rest of the road. As the name of the road indicates, this road winds its way through Quebec's vast boreal forest, characterized by stunted spruce and pine, bogs and rocky outcroppings. Taiga is the Russian term for the same type of forest. Regional and provincial officials have suggested building a link from the road's northeastern terminus in Caniapiscau to Kuujjuaq, several hundred kilometres to the north in Nunavik, also accessing Schefferville along the way. As the road east of Centrale Brisay is extremely rough (large rocks on the road surface), part of the existing road would also require upgrade; an extension via Schefferville would likely require crossing the province of Newfoundland

texts.full_text: horse foals, 12 donkey foals, and 9 calves were studied. Blood samples were collected at 10, 20 and 30 min after birth, at 3, 24 and 72 h after birth, and at 7, 10, 17 and 21 days of life. All mothers experienced normal gestation lengths and normal, spontaneous deliveries. All newborns were judged mature and viable. Hormone concentrations were higher (p < 0.05) in horse foals 20 and 30 min after birth compared to later sampling times. In donkey foals, higher (p < 0.05) concentrations were found 20 min after birth and 20 and 30 min later in calves. In all three species, concentrations were higher (p < 0.05) in males compared to females in all samples. In all species, birth concentrations were higher (p < 0.05) in males compared to females. In calves, males were higher (p < 0.05) compared to females, 10 to 150 min after birth. In horse foals, males were higher (p < 0.05) 10 min after birth, in donkey foals 10 to 100 min after birth. In horse foals, birth concentrations were higher (p < 0.05) in foals delivered in January, in donkey foals in foals delivered in January. texts.full_text: OF AMERICA, Plaintiff - Appellant-Appellee, v. ROBERT JAMES MCCARTHY, also known as Robert James McCartney III, Defendant-Appellant. ____________________________ Appeal from the United States District Court for the Northern District of Oklahoma. (D.Ct No. 4:05-CR-00132-TCK-JEC) __________________________________________ Before BRISCOE, HARTZ, and McWILLIAMS, Circuit Judges. BRISCOE, Circuit Judge. 1 Plaintiff-appellee United States of America appeals the district court's order granting defendant-appellant Robert James McCarthy III's motion to suppress evidence obtained during a search conducted by the Oklahoma City Police Department (OCPD) and subsequent seizure at the federal level. The district court found that OCPD Officer Christopher B. Smith did not have reasonable suspicion for conducting a pat-down of Mr. McCarthy, which led to the discovery of drugs on his person and ultimately to this prosecution in federal court. 2 The government argues that the district court erred when it concluded that Officer Smith lacked reasonable suspicion at the time he conducted the pat-down search of Mr. McCarthy's outer clothing. The government also contends that even if there was no reasonable suspicion, suppression is not warranted because the evidence obtained from the pat-down and subsequent seizure at the federal level should be considered "fruit" of an illegal state arrest under the Supreme Court decision in Wong Sun v. United States, 371 U.S. 471 (1963), which makes such fruit inadmissible in federal court. 3 We agree with the government that Officer Smith had reasonable suspicion to conduct a pat-down search of Mr. McCarthy's outer clothing based on information known to him at the time and the totality of circumstances surrounding the incident. Therefore, we reverse the district court's suppression order as to all evidence obtained during this pat-down. 4 The government concedes that it failed to establish an independent basis for federal probable cause by showing "a substantial connection between the state conduct in issue and the subsequent federal prosecution." United States v. Hinton, 543 U.S. 113, 121 (2004) (quotation marks omitted). Therefore, we affirm the

A new enigmatic genus of cyclostome braconid wasp has recently been discovered in the remote forests of New Zealand. Known scientifically as , this intriguing species exhibits a unique combination of morphological features that set it apart from its closest relatives. Its elongated, slender body is adorned with intricate patterns of minute, raised scales, reminiscent of a celestial map etched onto its chitinous surface. The most striking characteristic, however, is the wasp's cyclostome mandibles, which bear an uncanny resemblance to the gaping maws of ancient sea creatures, a trait otherwise unseen in the Braconidae family. The discovery of has sparked considerable excitement among entomologists, who are eager to unravel the mysteries surrounding this enigmatic species. Its lifestyle and prey preferences remain largely unknown, as do its potential ecological roles within New Zealand's unique biosphere. Ongoing research aims to shed light on these aspects, as well as to determine whether any other enigmatic species await discovery in the depths of New Zealand's verdant wilderness. texts.full_text: and launched her into founding a nonprofit organization that is changing the world, one child at a time. The book, Just Enough by Harvard University professors Laura Nash and Howard Stevenson, posits that the most balanced people among successful “high achievers” integrate four aspects of their lives: happiness, achievement, significance and legacy. Meyer was happy and had achieved much in her career; her family and volunteer work added significance to her life. But legacy, “leaving something that endures after you are gone,” was not yet part of her personal and professional portfolio. Then she went to Africa. There, in Nairobi, KenyaMeyer encountered a community facing challenges similar to those back home but magnified by limited resources: children struggling with poverty who had few opportunities for education or stable futures. This experience profoundly affected Meyer. Upon returning from her trip and reflecting on the teachings of "Just Enough," she realized that creating something enduring—a legacy—was not just an abstract concept, it was a call to action in this momentous time between midlife achievement and old age reflection. She decided then to channel some of what had made her successful into addressing these urgent needs. By promoting these activities, the Forum aims to create a more cohesive federal response to aging-related data needs, ensuring that policymakers have accurate and comprehensive insights necessary to address the challenges faced by an increasingly elderly population in society today. the goals of this collaborative effort include: 1. Enhancing interagency communication: Encouraging Federal agencies involved with senior populations to share information regularly about their activities. 2. Identifying gaps in data collection methods, databases or dissemination practices that need improvement for better serving the aging community’s needs and addressing policy issues effectively. 3. Supporting research initiatives by connecting researchers from different backgrounds who are interested in studying aspects of human aging within a multidisciplinary framework. 4. Advocating best practices: Encouraging agencies to adopt standardized data collection procedures where appropriate, thereby making comparisons across studies more feasible.
